prompt: A WELL v2 project team is designing open-plan office lighting to support circadian health. Which metric is most directly used to evaluate melanopic (circadian-effective) light exposure?

options.text: Equivalent Melanopic Lux (EML) at the eye
Total lumens per square foot on the workplane only
Color Rendering Index (CRI) of the lamp
Wattage per fixture regardless of spectrum

explanation: WELL Light concepts emphasize circadian-effective illumination evaluated at the occupant's eye using melanopic weighting, commonly expressed as EML rather than generic illuminance alone.

distractorExplanations.b: Horizontal workplane lux does not account for vertical eye-level exposure or melanopic spectrum weighting.
distractorExplanations.c: CRI describes color rendering quality, not circadian stimulus.
distractorExplanations.d: Wattage does not describe spectral content or retinal circadian response.

prompt: Which design strategy best supports WELL Movement concepts in a mid-rise office building?

options.text: Place attractive, visible stairs near main circulation and reduce reliance on elevators for short trips
Remove all interior stairs to simplify wayfinding
Require badge access to all stairwells during business hours
Locate all meeting rooms only on the ground floor

explanation: WELL Movement promotes active design by making stairs convenient, visible, and appealing so occupants choose them for everyday vertical travel.

distractorExplanations.b: Removing stairs eliminates a primary opportunity for daily physical activity.
distractorExplanations.c: Restricting stair access discourages routine use for movement.
distractorExplanations.d: Ground-floor-only meetings do not address vertical movement between floors.

prompt: Under ASHRAE Standard 55 thermal comfort principles referenced in WELL Thermal Comfort, what does PMV primarily indicate?

options.text: Predicted mean vote of thermal sensation on a comfort scale
Peak mechanical ventilation rate in cubic feet per minute
Particulate matter concentration in supply air
Primary motor voltage for fan equipment

explanation: PMV (Predicted Mean Vote) models average thermal sensation based on environmental and personal factors such as air temperature, radiant temperature, humidity, air speed, clothing, and metabolic rate.

distractorExplanations.b: PMV is a human comfort index, not an airflow quantity.
distractorExplanations.c: Particulate matter relates to Air concepts, not PMV.
distractorExplanations.d: Motor voltage is unrelated to thermal sensation modeling.

prompt: A project provides sit-stand workstations and interior walking loops. Which WELL concept area do these strategies primarily address?

options.text: Movement
Water
Sound
Materials

explanation: Active furnishings and interior walking paths reduce sedentary behavior and support WELL Movement goals for physical activity in daily building use.

distractorExplanations.b: Water features address hydration and water quality, not physical activity.
distractorExplanations.c: Sound addresses acoustic comfort and noise control.
distractorExplanations.d: Materials focuses on product ingredient transparency and VOC limits.

prompt: Which lighting control approach best reduces circadian disruption from bright artificial light at night in a 24/7 operations center?

options.text: Lower melanopic content and illuminance during night shifts while maintaining task visibility
Increase all fixtures to 6500 K and maximum output overnight
Disable all lighting controls and run at full brightness continuously
Use only decorative accent lighting with no task lighting

explanation: WELL Light strategies for shift workers include managing spectrum and intensity at night to limit circadian disruption while preserving safe task illumination.

distractorExplanations.b: High cool-temperature light at night can increase circadian disruption for night workers.
distractorExplanations.c: Constant maximum brightness ignores time-of-day circadian needs.
distractorExplanations.d: Insufficient task lighting compromises safety and performance.

prompt: A naturally ventilated office in a mild climate allows occupants to open windows. Which thermal comfort framework is often appropriate when occupants have adaptive control?

options.text: Adaptive comfort model allowing wider acceptable temperature ranges when users can adjust openings
Fixed 68°F setpoint with no occupant control year-round
Radiant heating only with no ventilation
Ignore humidity entirely in all seasons

explanation: Adaptive comfort models recognize that acceptable temperature ranges can broaden when occupants can open windows, adjust clothing, or otherwise adapt, as referenced in ASHRAE 55 and WELL Thermal Comfort.

distractorExplanations.b: Rigid setpoints without adaptive opportunity do not reflect operable-window environments.
distractorExplanations.c: Radiant-only systems do not describe adaptive comfort methodology.
distractorExplanations.d: Humidity remains a comfort factor even in adaptive models.

prompt: Which window treatment strategy best supports both daylight access and glare control for desk workers near perimeter glazing?

options.text: Automated or manual shades that block direct sun while preserving diffuse daylight
Permanent blackout panels on all windows at all times
No shades and maximum direct beam sunlight on monitors all day
Tinted glazing with zero operable shading regardless of sun angle

explanation: Effective daylight design balances access to natural light with glare control through shading that responds to sun angle, supporting WELL Light visual comfort.

distractorExplanations.b: Permanent blackout eliminates beneficial daylight.
distractorExplanations.c: Uncontrolled direct sun causes disability glare on screens.
distractorExplanations.d: Fixed tint alone may not address changing solar angles throughout the day.

prompt: A fitness amenity includes on-site exercise space accessible to building occupants. Which WELL domain does this primarily support?

options.text: Movement
Nourishment
Community
Air

explanation: Dedicated exercise facilities encourage regular physical activity, a core WELL Movement strategy beyond incidental stair use or walking.

distractorExplanations.b: Nourishment addresses healthy food environments, not structured exercise space.
distractorExplanations.c: Community may overlap socially but the primary intent is physical activity.
distractorExplanations.d: Air addresses ventilation and pollutant control, not exercise programming.

prompt: What is the primary purpose of measuring vertical illuminance at the eye for daylighting assessments in WELL projects?

options.text: To evaluate light available for visual and circadian effects at the occupant's line of sight
To size emergency generator fuel tanks
To calculate plumbing fixture counts
To determine structural wind loads on the facade

explanation: Eye-level vertical illuminance reflects what enters the eye for vision and circadian stimulation, which is more relevant than floor-level metrics alone for WELL Light evaluation.

distractorExplanations.b: Generator sizing is unrelated to daylight measurement.
distractorExplanations.c: Plumbing counts are a Water concept.
distractorExplanations.d: Wind loads are structural, not lighting metrics.

prompt: A building operator receives frequent hot-cold complaints in one open office zone despite a central thermostat reading 72°F. What is the best first diagnostic step aligned with thermal comfort management?

options.text: Survey occupants and check local radiant asymmetry, air speed, and solar gains at complaint locations
Lower the global setpoint to 65°F for the entire building
Disable the BAS and lock out all HVAC indefinitely
Ignore complaints because the thermostat reading is within range

explanation: Thermal comfort depends on multiple factors beyond air temperature at one sensor; localized radiant conditions, drafts, and solar load often explain zone complaints under WELL Thermal Comfort principles.

distractorExplanations.b: Global setpoint changes may worsen comfort elsewhere without diagnosing root cause.
distractorExplanations.c: Shutting down HVAC creates larger comfort and health problems.
distractorExplanations.d: A single sensor reading does not represent all occupants' thermal experience.

prompt: Which color temperature strategy during daytime hours generally supports alertness in office environments while maintaining visual comfort?

options.text: Neutral to cool-white spectrum with adequate melanopic content during working hours
Very warm 2200 K dim light at maximum desk illuminance all day
Monochromatic red lighting only
Random flickering between color temperatures every minute

explanation: Daytime lighting with appropriate spectrum and intensity supports visual performance and circadian entrainment; WELL Light encourages designed day-night contrast rather than flat warm dim light all day.

distractorExplanations.b: Very warm low-quality spectrum all day weakens daytime circadian signal.
distractorExplanations.c: Monochromatic red light is not suitable for general office tasks.
distractorExplanations.d: Rapid random changes create discomfort and poor lighting quality.

prompt: A project installs bike storage and showers near the building entrance. Which combination of WELL concepts do these amenities most directly support?

options.text: Movement and related active transportation support
Sound and Materials only
Water quality testing only
WELL Portfolio score aggregation only

explanation: Bicycle infrastructure and changing facilities reduce barriers to active commuting, aligning with WELL Movement strategies for physical activity in daily life.

distractorExplanations.b: Acoustic and material strategies are unrelated to bike commuting infrastructure.
distractorExplanations.c: Water testing does not describe active transportation amenities.
distractorExplanations.d: Portfolio scoring is a certification process topic, not the primary concept for bike facilities.

prompt: Relative humidity in an office is consistently 18% during winter heating. Which occupant impact is most likely?

options.text: Dry air discomfort affecting eyes and respiratory mucous membranes
Increased structural wind uplift on the roof
Automatic WELL precertification approval
Elimination of all thermal comfort complaints

explanation: Very low winter humidity contributes to thermal comfort and health-related dryness complaints; WELL Thermal Comfort considers humidity among environmental comfort factors.

distractorExplanations.b: Humidity does not directly change structural wind uplift.
distractorExplanations.c: Humidity alone does not determine certification outcomes.
distractorExplanations.d: Low humidity typically increases comfort complaints, not eliminates them.

prompt: Which metric pair is commonly used together to describe thermal environment acceptability under ASHRAE 55?

options.text: PMV and PPD (Predicted Percentage Dissatisfied)
STC and IIC
MERV and HEPA
LEED and WELL

explanation: PMV estimates mean thermal sensation while PPD estimates the percentage of occupants likely dissatisfied, forming the standard thermal comfort evaluation pair in ASHRAE 55.

distractorExplanations.b: STC and IIC are acoustic transmission ratings for Sound concepts.
distractorExplanations.c: MERV and HEPA describe filtration, not thermal sensation.
distractorExplanations.d: LEED and WELL are rating systems, not thermal comfort metrics.

prompt: A designer locates photocell sensors inside a deep floor plate far from windows. What lighting control issue is most likely?

options.text: Daylight harvesting will dim lights incorrectly because sensors do not see perimeter daylight conditions
Stair pressurization will fail
Water legionella risk increases automatically
WELL exam pass score changes

explanation: Daylight sensors must be placed where they represent the daylight available to the controlled zone; poor sensor location causes inappropriate dimming and energy or comfort problems.

distractorExplanations.b: Stair pressurization is life-safety mechanical design, unrelated to photocell placement.
distractorExplanations.c: Legionella is a Water concept unrelated to lighting sensors.
distractorExplanations.d: Exam scoring is independent of project sensor placement.

prompt: Which WELL Light strategy helps occupants who receive limited daylight due to interior office locations?

options.text: Provide high-quality electric lighting with appropriate spectrum, intensity, and glare control at workstations
Remove all electric lighting in interior zones
Require all interior staff to relocate to the roof daily
Use only candlelight for all task work

explanation: When daylight is unavailable, designed electric lighting can still support visual performance and circadian health through appropriate intensity, spectrum, and control.

distractorExplanations.b: Removing electric light leaves interior zones under-lit and non-functional.
distractorExplanations.c: Roof relocation is not a practical design strategy.
distractorExplanations.d: Candlelight cannot meet office task or circadian lighting requirements.

prompt: An office policy encourages walking meetings in interior circulation paths. Which WELL concept does this operational strategy support?

options.text: Movement
Materials
Mind
Sound

explanation: Walking meetings increase daily physical activity during work hours, supporting WELL Movement beyond built infrastructure alone.

distractorExplanations.b: Materials addresses product chemistry and transparency.
distractorExplanations.c: Mind focuses on mental health and cognitive support; walking meetings primarily increase activity.
distractorExplanations.d: Sound addresses acoustic environment quality.

prompt: Which condition most likely causes asymmetric thermal discomfort near a large exterior glass wall on a sunny winter day?

options.text: Radiant temperature difference from solar-heated glazing while air temperature remains moderate
Excessive plumbing vent stack height
Low MERV filter rating in a remote mechanical room only
Missing bike room signage

explanation: Radiant asymmetry from warm or cold surfaces is a common thermal comfort issue; occupants may feel hot near sunlit glass even when air temperature seems acceptable.

distractorExplanations.b: Plumbing vent height does not create radiant asymmetry at desks.
distractorExplanations.c: Remote filter rating does not explain localized radiant discomfort at glazing.
distractorExplanations.d: Signage is unrelated to thermal radiation from windows.

prompt: A project team specifies tunable-white luminaires. What is the primary WELL-related benefit?

options.text: Ability to adjust spectrum and intensity across the day to support circadian and visual needs
Elimination of all electrical energy use
Automatic compliance with all Water features
Replacement of all WELL documentation requirements

explanation: Tunable-white systems allow programmed or manual shifts in color temperature and output to create day-evening contrast aligned with WELL Light circadian design.

distractorExplanations.b: Tunable luminaires still consume electricity.
distractorExplanations.c: Lighting technology does not satisfy Water prerequisites.
distractorExplanations.d: Documentation and verification remain required for WELL certification.

prompt: Which personal factor in thermal comfort models accounts for activity level such as seated office work versus walking?

options.text: Metabolic rate (met)
Sound transmission class
Community engagement score
Filter pressure drop

explanation: Metabolic rate describes heat generated by human activity and is a key input in PMV/PPD and adaptive comfort assessments under ASHRAE 55.

distractorExplanations.b: STC is an acoustic property of assemblies.
distractorExplanations.c: Community engagement is a WELL Community concept, not a thermal model input.
distractorExplanations.d: Filter pressure drop relates to HVAC filtration, not occupant metabolism.

prompt: A ground-floor retail tenant with high internal loads feels warm while upper office floors feel cold in a single-air-system building. What thermal comfort issue is most likely?

options.text: Zone load diversity and inadequate local control for dissimilar occupancies
Incorrect WELL AP exam registration fee
Missing universal design signage only
Excessive sound masking in retail only

explanation: Mixed-use buildings often need zoning or supplemental control because internal gains and occupancy patterns differ; one global condition rarely satisfies all spaces.

distractorExplanations.b: Exam fees are unrelated to HVAC zoning.
distractorExplanations.c: Universal design signage does not explain temperature imbalance.
distractorExplanations.d: Sound masking does not cause inter-zonal temperature differences.

prompt: Which daylight performance metric focuses on the percentage of floor area meeting minimum daylight illuminance thresholds?

options.text: Spatial Daylight Autonomy (sDA) or similar area-based daylight metrics
Occupant load factor for egress only
Water flow rate in gallons per flush
Portfolio recertification interval

explanation: Area-based daylight metrics such as sDA evaluate how much regularly occupied floor area achieves useful daylight levels, supporting WELL Light design verification.

distractorExplanations.b: Egress occupant load is a life-safety calculation.
distractorExplanations.c: Flush volume is a Water fixture metric.
distractorExplanations.d: Recertification timing belongs to WELL Certification concepts.

prompt: A project provides clear signage and attractive finishes in stairwells. Which dual benefit supports WELL goals?

options.text: Encourages stair use (Movement) and improves perceived safety and comfort in circulation
Eliminates need for any ventilation testing
Replaces all nourishment labeling requirements
Guarantees WELL Platinum without documentation

explanation: Well-designed, visible stairs increase daily physical activity while making circulation spaces feel secure and welcoming, supporting Movement and overall occupant experience.

distractorExplanations.b: Ventilation testing remains independent of stair aesthetics.
distractorExplanations.c: Nourishment labeling is a separate WELL concept.
distractorExplanations.d: Certification level requires documented compliance, not finishes alone.

prompt: Which clothing insulation factor is commonly represented as 'clo' in thermal comfort calculations?

options.text: A unit describing insulating value of occupant clothing ensembles
A measure of water turbidity in drinking fountains
A sound absorption coefficient for ceiling tiles
A WELL feature point weighting

explanation: Clo quantifies clothing insulation in thermal comfort models; seasonal dress expectations affect acceptable temperature ranges in PMV analysis.

distractorExplanations.b: Turbidity is a water quality parameter.
distractorExplanations.c: Absorption coefficients describe acoustics, not clothing.
distractorExplanations.d: Feature weighting is part of WELL scoring structure, not a thermal unit.

prompt: An employer implements a policy allowing flexible dress by season to match indoor temperature conditions. Which thermal comfort principle does this support?

options.text: Personal adaptation through clothing adjustment within acceptable environmental limits
Elimination of all HVAC maintenance
Mandatory uniform heavy coats in summer
Removal of all WELL Movement features

explanation: Allowing seasonal clothing flexibility helps occupants adapt to indoor conditions, complementing environmental controls under thermal comfort best practices.

distractorExplanations.b: HVAC maintenance remains essential regardless of dress code.
distractorExplanations.c: Heavy coats in summer hinder adaptation and comfort.
distractorExplanations.d: Dress policy does not remove Movement design features.
